---
title: '<img src="assets/logo.png" height="50"> Course Title'
subtitle: "Course Skeleton"
author: "Instructor Name"
institution: "Name of Institution"
format: html
---

This is a generic course landing page.

Here students will find:

- a course [syllabus](syllabus.html) (edit this in `syllabus.qmd`)

- A tentative schedule; see @tbl-sched


## Course Schedule

The schedule below is tentative and may be adjusted as the course progresses.

| Week | Topic | Materials |
|-----------------------:|:-----------------------|------------------------|
| 1 | Introduction to Quarto Slides with webr | [slides](lectures/lecture-template.html) |
| 2 | Linear Regression demo | [slides](lectures/regression-lecture.html) |

: *The tentative course schedule* {#tbl-sched}


## Lecture Slide

The slide decks above were made in Quarto in revealjs (HTML). A lecture template with some helpful demonstrations can be [here](lectures/lecture-template.qmd); see the rendered version [here](https://irene.vrbik.ok.ubc.ca/webr-demo/lectures/lecture-template.html#/title-slide).  

::: {.callout-note}
Note that revealjs presentations can be presented as HTML slides or can be printed to PDF for easier distribution. 🎉
:::


### Print/Save to PDF:

Reveal presentations can be exported to PDF via a special print stylesheet.

1.  Toggle into Print View using the `E` key (or using the [Navigation Menu](https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/presenting.html#navigation-menu))
2.  Open the in-browser[^2] print dialog (CTRL/CMD+P).
3.  Change the **Destination** setting to **Save as PDF**.
4.  Change the **Layout** to **Landscape**.
5.  Change the **Margins** to **None**.
6.  Enable the **Background graphics** option.
7.  Click **Save** 🎉



Visit <https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/> for more details on create revealjs slides.
